original_hypothesis.title: cook() Solvency Check Bypass via Unhandled Action Codes
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The cook() function's solvency check can be entirely bypassed by appending an unhandled action code (e.g., ACTION_ACCRUE=8, or any value like 0, 1, 3, 9) after ACTION_BORROW or ACTION_REMOVE_COLLATERAL. The unhandled action falls to the else branch calling _additionalCookAction(), which returns a default-initialized CookStatus{needsSolvencyCheck:false, hasAccrued:false}. The line `status = returnStatus` overwrites the previously set needsSolvencyCheck=true, causing the final solvency check to be skipped entirely.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a direct code defect in the most critical financial control of the lending protocol. The solvency check is the sole mechanism preventing undercollateralized borrowing. Its bypass enables unlimited borrowing against minimal or no collateral, draining the entire MIM supply from the Cauldron.

proof_of_signal.pattern: else branch calls _additionalCookAction which returns default CookStatus; status = returnStatus overwrites needsSolvencyCheck=true set by ACTION_BORROW

preconditions: Any CauldronV4 or BlastCauldronV4 clone is deployed and funded with MIM
Attacker has some collateral in BentoBox or uses skim mode
No override of _additionalCookAction that preserves CookStatus

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: status = returnStatus where returnStatus is default CookStatus{false,false} from empty _additionalCookAction
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: cook([ACTION_BORROW, 0], ...) - second action is unhandled, resets needsSolvencyCheck to false
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker borrows entire MIM balance of Cauldron with minimal collateral

economic_questions: What is the total MIM supply available across all deployed Cauldron clones?
Does BlastCauldronV4 or any other subclass override _additionalCookAction to fix this?
Can the attack be executed atomically in a single transaction?
validation_requirements: Construct PoC: cook([5,0], [0,0], [borrow_abi_data, 0x]) and verify no solvency check
Verify _additionalCookAction returns default CookStatus in CauldronV4 base
Confirm ACTION_ACCRUE(8) has no handler in the if-else chain

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Minimal collateral (1 wei of collateral share sufficient)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Entire MIM balance of targeted Cauldron clone

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time while Cauldron has MIM
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete drain of Cauldron MIM supply

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All Cauldron MIM depositors and the protocol treasury
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% loss of MIM lent to Cauldron
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All MIM in all CauldronV4 clones

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: ACTION_ACCRUE(8) defined but unhandled; actions 0,1,3,9 also unhandled
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace CookStatus through cook() with [ACTION_BORROW, 0] sequence

validation_result.mechanism: CookStatus overwrite via unhandled action in cook()
validation_result.impact_summary: Any user can bypass the solvency check after borrowing or removing collateral by appending an unhandled action code (e.g., 8) to the cook() actions array, enabling unlimited undercollateralized borrowing and complete drain of Cauldron MIM.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The cook() function in CauldronV4.sol maintains a `CookStatus memory status` struct to track whether a solvency check is needed after the batch of actions completes. When ACTION_BORROW (5) or ACTION_REMOVE_COLLATERAL (4) is executed, `status.needsSolvencyCheck` is set to `true`. However, when an unhandled action code falls to the `else` branch, `_additionalCookAction()` is called. This virtual function has an empty body in CauldronV4 and returns default-initialized values, including `CookStatus{needsSolvencyCheck: false, hasAccrued: false}`. The line `status = returnStatus` then completely overwrites the existing status struct, resetting `needsSolvencyCheck` to `false`. The final solvency check `if (status.needsSolvencyCheck)` evaluates to `false` and the check is entirely skipped. ACTION_ACCRUE (8) is defined as a constant but has no handler in the if-else chain, making it a convenient unhandled action code. Actions 0, 1, 3, 9, and any value not matching a handler also trigger this path. BlastCauldronV4 does not override `_additionalCookAction`, so it inherits the vulnerable base implementation.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof-of-signal is correct. Lines 319-365 of CauldronV4.sol contain the cook() function. The else branch at the end of the if-else chain calls `_additionalCookAction` and overwrites `status` with the return value. The `_additionalCookAction` function at line ~312 has an empty body returning default CookStatus{false, false}. ACTION_ACCRUE=8 is defined at line ~292 but has no handler in the if-else chain. The overwrite happens unconditionally regardless of the prior state of `status.needsSolvencyCheck`.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet: function _additionalCookAction(uint8 action, CookStatus memory, uint256 value, bytes memory data, uint256 value1, uint256 value2) internal virtual returns (bytes memory, uint8, CookStatus memory) {}
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Empty function body returns default CookStatus{needsSolvencyCheck: false, hasAccrued: false}. The second parameter (current status) is unnamed and ignored, so the returned status is always zeroed.

The line `status = returnStatus` unconditionally overwrites the entire CookStatus struct, including needsSolvencyCheck which may have been set to true by a prior ACTION_BORROW or ACTION_REMOVE_COLLATERAL.
The solvency check is conditional on status.needsSolvencyCheck, which has been reset to false by the unhandled action's return.

calling_sequence: 4. _borrow(to, amount) executes, MIM transferred to attacker
8. action=8 matches no if-else branch, falls to else
15. Function returns successfully, attacker has borrowed MIM without solvency verification
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: cook() → ACTION_BORROW sets needsSolvencyCheck=true → unhandled action 8 falls to else branch → _additionalCookAction returns default CookStatus{false,false} → status = returnStatus overwrites needsSolvencyCheck to false → final solvency check is skipped

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Blast.s.sol deploys BlastCauldronV4 which inherits CauldronV4 without overriding _additionalCookAction. The vulnerability exists in the base contract and all subclasses that don't override _additionalCookAction to preserve the status.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack is straightforward and atomic: 1) Attacker deposits minimal collateral via addCollateral or skim. 2) Attacker calls cook([ACTION_ADD_COLLATERAL, ACTION_BORROW, 8], ...) or cook([ACTION_BORROW, 8], ...). The ACTION_BORROW borrows a large amount of MIM. Action 8 (unhandled) overwrites status, bypassing the solvency check. 3) Attacker receives MIM without any solvency verification. The attack requires only a single transaction and no special preconditions beyond having a funded Cauldron clone.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL. The solvency check is the sole mechanism preventing undercollateralized borrowing in Cauldrons. Its complete bypass allows an attacker to borrow the entire MIM balance of any CauldronV4 or BlastCauldronV4 clone with minimal or zero effective collateral. This would create massive bad debt and drain protocol funds. All deployed CauldronV4 clones and BlastCauldronV4 clones are affected.
recommended_mitigations: Change the else branch to preserve existing status flags: instead of `status = returnStatus`, use `if (returnStatus.needsSolvencyCheck) status.needsSolvencyCheck = true; if (returnStatus.hasAccrued) status.hasAccrued = true;` or use bitwise OR semantics.
Alternatively, pass the status by reference rather than by value and require _additionalCookAction to only set flags, never clear them.
Add a revert for truly unhandled action codes that are not in the ACTION_CUSTOM_START_INDEX range.
Consider making _additionalCookAction revert by default instead of returning silently with defaults.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a CauldronV4 clone with collateral and MIM in BentoBox. 2. Deposit 1 wei of collateral share for the attacker. 3. Call cook([5, 8], [0, 0], [abi.encode(int256(large_amount), attacker_address), bytes(0x)]) where large_amount exceeds what the collateral supports. 4. Verify the transaction succeeds (no solvency revert). 5. Verify attacker received MIM and the Cauldron has bad debt.
uncertainties: Whether any deployed subclass of CauldronV4 overrides _additionalCookAction to fix this (none found in scope).
The exact MIM balance available in deployed Cauldron clones (determines total extractable value).
additional_testing_needed: Forge test PoC executing the exact attack sequence cook([5, 8], ...) and verifying solvency check is skipped
Verify behavior with other unhandled action codes (0, 1, 3, 9) to confirm they all trigger the same path
Test with ACTION_REMOVE_COLLATERAL followed by unhandled action to confirm collateral removal also bypasses solvency
